---
title: "anova_stats"
output: html_notebook
---


one-way anova statistical analysis

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

cellular <- read.csv("cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
soluble  <- read.csv("soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")

out_dir <- "holistic_proteomics"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

get_sample_cols <- function(df) grep("Quantity\\.log2$", colnames(df), value = TRUE)
get_timepoint   <- function(col) sub("\\..*$", "", col)

run_anova <- function(df, fraction_name) {

  sample_cols <- get_sample_cols(df)
  timepoints  <- factor(get_timepoint(sample_cols),
                        levels = c("D14","D21","M03","M06","M12","M18"))

  mat <- as.matrix(df[, sample_cols])
  rownames(mat) <- df$gene_symbol
  mat[mat == 0] <- NA

  keep <- rowSums(!is.na(mat)) >= 3
  mat  <- mat[keep, ]

  mat_filled <- t(apply(mat, 1, function(x) {
    x[is.na(x)] <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    x
  }))

  anova_results <- apply(mat_filled, 1, function(x) {
    fit <- tryCatch(aov(x ~ timepoints), error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(fit)) return(c(f_stat = NA, p_value = NA))
    s <- summary(fit)[[1]]
    c(f_stat   = s["timepoints", "F value"],
      p_value  = s["timepoints", "Pr(>F)"])
  })

  result_df <- as.data.frame(t(anova_results)) %>%
    rownames_to_column("gene_symbol") %>%
    mutate(
      padj           = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"),
      is_significant = !is.na(padj) & padj < 0.05,
      fraction       = fraction_name
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(padj)

  write.csv(result_df,
            file.path(out_dir, paste0(tolower(fraction_name), "_anova_results.csv")),
            row.names = FALSE)

  sig <- result_df %>% dplyr::filter(is_significant)
  message(fraction_name, " - total proteins tested: ", nrow(result_df))
  message(fraction_name, " - significant (padj < 0.05): ", nrow(sig))

  result_df
}

cellular_anova <- run_anova(cellular, "cellular")
soluble_anova  <- run_anova(soluble,  "soluble")

combined <- bind_rows(cellular_anova, soluble_anova)
write.csv(combined, file.path(out_dir, "combined_anova_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

heatmap



trajectory plots and clusters

```{r}
install.packages("factoextra")
library(tidyverse)
library(cluster)     # silhouette
library(factoextra)  # fviz_nbclust, silhouette convenience

# =====================================================================
# 0. Load ANOVA results + original proteomics data (for timepoint values)
# =====================================================================
anova_cellular  <- read.csv("holistic_proteomics/cellular_anova_results.csv")
anova_soluble   <- read.csv("holistic_proteomics/soluble_anova_results.csv")
anova_insoluble <- read.csv("holistic_proteomics/insoluble_anova_results.csv")

cellular  <- read.csv("cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
soluble   <- read.csv("soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
insoluble <- read.csv("insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")

timepoint_cols <- c("mean_log2_D14", "mean_log2_D21", "mean_log2_M03",
                     "mean_log2_M06", "mean_log2_M12", "mean_log2_M18")

fraction_colours <- c("Cellular" = "#0072B2", "Soluble" = "#E69F00", "Insoluble" = "#009E73")

# =====================================================================
# 1. Build significant-gene trajectory matrices, z-scored row-wise
# =====================================================================
build_traj_matrix <- function(anova_df, proteomics_df, fraction_name) {
  sig_genes <- anova_df %>% filter(is_significant == TRUE) %>% pull(gene_symbol)

  traj <- proteomics_df %>%
    filter(gene_symbol %in% sig_genes) %>%
    filter(!is.na(gene_symbol)) %>%
    distinct(gene_symbol, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    select(gene_symbol, all_of(timepoint_cols)) %>%
    filter(complete.cases(.))   # k-means needs complete rows

  mat <- traj %>% select(-gene_symbol) %>% as.matrix()
  rownames(mat) <- traj$gene_symbol

  # row-wise z-score (standardise each protein's own trajectory)
  mat_z <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  mat_z <- mat_z[complete.cases(mat_z), ]  # drop zero-variance rows (all NaN after scaling)

  cat(fraction_name, ": ", nrow(mat_z), "significant proteins with complete trajectories\n")
  mat_z
}

mat_cellular  <- build_traj_matrix(anova_cellular,  cellular,  "Cellular")
mat_soluble   <- build_traj_matrix(anova_soluble,   soluble,   "Soluble")
mat_insoluble <- build_traj_matrix(anova_insoluble, insoluble, "Insoluble")

# =====================================================================
# 2. Choose optimal k via silhouette score, then run k-means
# =====================================================================
choose_k_and_cluster <- function(mat_z, fraction_name, k_range = 2:8, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)

  sil_scores <- map_dbl(k_range, function(k) {
    km <- kmeans(mat_z, centers = k, nstart = 25)
    ss <- silhouette(km$cluster, dist(mat_z))
    mean(ss[, 3])
  })

  sil_df <- tibble(k = k_range, avg_silhouette = sil_scores)
  best_k <- sil_df$k[which.max(sil_df$avg_silhouette)]

  cat(fraction_name, ": optimal k =", best_k,
      "(silhouette =", round(max(sil_scores), 3), ")\n")

  set.seed(seed)
  km_final <- kmeans(mat_z, centers = best_k, nstart = 25)

  list(sil_df = sil_df, best_k = best_k, kmeans_fit = km_final)
}

res_cellular  <- choose_k_and_cluster(mat_cellular,  "Cellular")
res_soluble   <- choose_k_and_cluster(mat_soluble,   "Soluble")
res_insoluble <- choose_k_and_cluster(mat_insoluble, "Insoluble")

# ---- optional: silhouette diagnostic plot per fraction ----
plot_silhouette <- function(res, fraction_name) {
  ggplot(res$sil_df, aes(x = k, y = avg_silhouette)) +
    geom_line(colour = fraction_colours[fraction_name], linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, colour = fraction_colours[fraction_name]) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = res$best_k, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    labs(title = paste(fraction_name, "- silhouette score by k"),
         x = "Number of clusters (k)", y = "Average silhouette width") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
}

plot_silhouette(res_cellular,  "Cellular")
plot_silhouette(res_soluble,   "Soluble")
plot_silhouette(res_insoluble, "Insoluble")

# =====================================================================
# 3. Reshape trajectories to long format for plotting
# =====================================================================
make_long_traj <- function(mat_z, km_fit, fraction_name) {
  as.data.frame(mat_z) %>%
    rownames_to_column("gene_symbol") %>%
    mutate(cluster = factor(km_fit$cluster[gene_symbol]),
           Fraction = fraction_name) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(timepoint_cols),
                 names_to = "Timepoint", values_to = "z_score") %>%
    mutate(Timepoint = gsub("mean_log2_", "", Timepoint),
           Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("D14","D21","M03","M06","M12","M18")),
           Timepoint_numeric = as.numeric(Timepoint))  # loess needs a numeric x
}

long_cellular  <- make_long_traj(mat_cellular,  res_cellular$kmeans_fit,  "Cellular")
long_soluble   <- make_long_traj(mat_soluble,   res_soluble$kmeans_fit,   "Soluble")
long_insoluble <- make_long_traj(mat_insoluble, res_insoluble$kmeans_fit, "Insoluble")

# =====================================================================
# 4. Plot trajectories per cluster: individual proteins (thin, faint)
#    + loess-smoothed cluster mean (thick)
# =====================================================================
plot_trajectories <- function(long_df, fraction_name, n_clusters) {
  ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Timepoint_numeric, y = z_score, group = gene_symbol)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.08, colour = "grey50") +
    geom_smooth(aes(group = cluster, colour = cluster), method = "loess",
                se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.3, span = 0.75) +
    facet_wrap(~cluster, ncol = n_clusters, labeller = label_both) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6,
                        labels = c("D14","D21","M03","M06","M12","M18")) +
    labs(title = paste(fraction_name, "- protein trajectory clusters"),
         subtitle = paste("k =", n_clusters, "(silhouette-selected); loess-smoothed cluster trend"),
         x = "Timepoint", y = "Row-wise z-scored log2 abundance") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}

p_traj_cellular  <- plot_trajectories(long_cellular,  "Cellular",  res_cellular$best_k)
p_traj_soluble   <- plot_trajectories(long_soluble,   "Soluble",   res_soluble$best_k)
p_traj_insoluble <- plot_trajectories(long_insoluble, "Insoluble", res_insoluble$best_k)

p_traj_cellular
p_traj_soluble
p_traj_insoluble

# =====================================================================
# 5. Save outputs
# =====================================================================
ggsave("trajectory_clusters_cellular.pdf",  p_traj_cellular,  width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave("trajectory_clusters_soluble.pdf",   p_traj_soluble,   width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave("trajectory_clusters_insoluble.pdf", p_traj_insoluble, width = 12, height = 5)

# cluster membership tables, useful for downstream enrichment
write.csv(long_cellular  %>% distinct(gene_symbol, cluster), "cellular_cluster_membership.csv",  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(long_soluble   %>% distinct(gene_symbol, cluster), "soluble_cluster_membership.csv",   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(long_insoluble %>% distinct(gene_symbol, cluster), "insoluble_cluster_membership.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```




---
title: "trajectory_and_enrichment_figures"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(rrvgo)
library(GO.db)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)   # mouse; matches gprofiler2 organism = "mmusculus"
library(patchwork)
```

## 0. Dissertation theme and colour palettes

```{r theme}
dissertation_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Carlito") +
  theme(
    plot.title        = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5, colour = "black"),
    plot.subtitle     = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 10, hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey30"),
    strip.text        = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 10, colour = "black"),
    strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.6),
    panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.border      = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
    axis.title        = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 11, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 9, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.y       = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 9.5, lineheight = 0.9, colour = "black"),
    legend.title      = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 10, colour = "black"),
    legend.text       = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 9, colour = "black"),
    legend.position   = "none"
  )

fraction_colours <- c("Cellular" = "#0072B2", "Soluble" = "#E69F00", "Insoluble" = "#009E73")
cluster_palette  <- c("#0072B2", "#E69F00", "#009E73", "#D55E00",
                       "#CC79A7", "#F0E442", "#56B4E9", "#999999")
```

## 1. Trajectory plots (per fraction)

```{r trajectory-function}
plot_fraction_trajectories <- function(long_df, fraction_name, n_clusters) {
  ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Timepoint_numeric, y = z_score)) +
    geom_line(aes(group = gene_symbol), alpha = 0.07, colour = "grey40") +
    geom_smooth(aes(group = cluster), method = "loess", se = TRUE,
                linewidth = 1.2, span = 0.75,
                colour = fraction_colours[fraction_name],
                fill = fraction_colours[fraction_name], alpha = 0.15) +
    facet_wrap(~cluster, nrow = 1, labeller = label_both) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6,
                        labels = c("D14","D21","M03","M06","M12","M18")) +
    labs(
      title = fraction_name,
      subtitle = paste0("k = ", n_clusters, " (silhouette-selected)"),
      x = "Timepoint", y = "z-scored log2 abundance"
    ) +
    dissertation_theme +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold", size = 8))
}
```

```{r trajectory-plots}
# Requires long_cellular, long_soluble, long_insoluble and
# res_cellular$best_k, res_soluble$best_k, res_insoluble$best_k from clustering step
p_traj_cellular  <- plot_fraction_trajectories(long_cellular,  "Cellular",  res_cellular$best_k)
p_traj_soluble   <- plot_fraction_trajectories(long_soluble,   "Soluble",   res_soluble$best_k)
p_traj_insoluble <- plot_fraction_trajectories(long_insoluble, "Insoluble", res_insoluble$best_k)

p_traj_cellular
p_traj_soluble
p_traj_insoluble
```

## 2. Enrichment term reduction

```{r enrichment-normalise}
# Case/hyphenation normalisation + dedup
# (fixes "Base excision repair" vs "base-excision repair" appearing as two bars)
prep_enrichment_for_reduction <- function(enrich_df) {
  enrich_df %>%
    mutate(
      term_name_norm = str_to_lower(term_name),
      term_name_norm = str_replace_all(term_name_norm, "-", " ")
    )
}

dedupe_case_variants <- function(enrich_df) {
  enrich_df %>%
    group_by(cluster, source, term_name_norm) %>%
    slice_min(order_by = p_value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup()
}

enrich_cellular  <- enrich_cellular  %>% prep_enrichment_for_reduction() %>% dedupe_case_variants()
enrich_soluble   <- enrich_soluble   %>% prep_enrichment_for_reduction() %>% dedupe_case_variants()
enrich_insoluble <- enrich_insoluble %>% prep_enrichment_for_reduction() %>% dedupe_case_variants()
```

```{r}
# rrvgo redundancy reduction — GO:BP only, per cluster
# KEGG terms pass through untouched (already deduplicated above)
reduce_go_redundancy <- function(enrich_df, fraction_name, threshold = 0.7) {
  clusters <- sort(unique(enrich_df$cluster))

  map_dfr(clusters, function(cl) {
    cluster_df <- enrich_df %>% filter(cluster == cl)

    go_df   <- cluster_df %>% filter(source == "GO:BP")
    kegg_df <- cluster_df %>% filter(source == "KEGG")

    if (nrow(go_df) < 2) {
      return(bind_rows(go_df, kegg_df))
    }

    simMatrix <- tryCatch(
      calculateSimMatrix(go_df$term_id,
                          orgdb = "org.Mm.eg.db",
                          ont = "BP",
                          method = "Rel"),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(simMatrix) || nrow(simMatrix) < 2) {
      cat(fraction_name, "- Cluster", cl, ": simMatrix failed, keeping all GO terms\n")
      return(bind_rows(go_df, kegg_df))
    }

    scores <- setNames(-log10(go_df$p_value), go_df$term_id)
    scores <- scores[rownames(simMatrix)]

    reducedTerms <- reduceSimMatrix(simMatrix, scores,
                                     threshold = threshold,
                                     orgdb = "org.Mm.eg.db")

    parent_ids <- unique(reducedTerms$parent)
    go_df_reduced <- go_df %>% filter(term_id %in% parent_ids)

    cat(fraction_name, "- Cluster", cl, ": GO:BP", nrow(go_df),
        "->", nrow(go_df_reduced), "terms after redundancy reduction\n")

    bind_rows(go_df_reduced, kegg_df)
  })
}

enrich_cellular_reduced  <- reduce_go_redundancy(enrich_cellular,  "Cellular")
enrich_soluble_reduced   <- reduce_go_redundancy(enrich_soluble,   "Soluble")
enrich_insoluble_reduced <- reduce_go_redundancy(enrich_insoluble, "Insoluble")
```

```{r}
# Re-score by temporal change magnitude on the reduced term sets
plot_cellular  <- score_terms_by_magnitude(enrich_cellular_reduced,  magnitude_cellular,  n_top = 10)
plot_soluble   <- score_terms_by_magnitude(enrich_soluble_reduced,   magnitude_soluble,   n_top = 10)
plot_insoluble <- score_terms_by_magnitude(enrich_insoluble_reduced, magnitude_insoluble, n_top = 10)
```

```{r}
# Flatten list-columns first (fixes "unimplemented type 'list'" error from write.csv)
flatten_list_cols <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.list), ~map_chr(.x, ~paste(unlist(.x), collapse = ";"))))
}

write.csv(flatten_list_cols(enrich_cellular_reduced),  "cellular_enrichment_reduced.csv",  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(flatten_list_cols(enrich_soluble_reduced),   "soluble_enrichment_reduced.csv",   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(flatten_list_cols(enrich_insoluble_reduced), "insoluble_enrichment_reduced.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

## 3. Enrichment barplots (per fraction)

```{r}
make_flat_enrichment_barplot <- function(plot_df, fraction_name, wrap_width = 40) {
  n_clusters <- length(unique(plot_df$cluster))
  pal <- setNames(cluster_palette[1:n_clusters], levels(factor(plot_df$cluster)))

  plot_df <- plot_df %>%
    mutate(
      cluster = factor(cluster),
      term_label = str_wrap(term_name, width = wrap_width)
    ) %>%
    arrange(cluster, desc(mean_magnitude)) %>%
    mutate(term_label = fct_inorder(term_label))

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = fct_rev(term_label), y = mean_magnitude, fill = cluster)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Cluster",
                       labels = paste("Cluster", levels(plot_df$cluster))) +
    labs(
      title = fraction_name,
      x = NULL, y = "Mean temporal change (log2 range)"
    ) +
    dissertation_theme +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}
```

```{r}
p_enrich_cellular  <- make_flat_enrichment_barplot(plot_cellular,  "Cellular")
p_enrich_soluble   <- make_flat_enrichment_barplot(plot_soluble,   "Soluble")
p_enrich_insoluble <- make_flat_enrichment_barplot(plot_insoluble, "Insoluble")

p_enrich_cellular
p_enrich_soluble
p_enrich_insoluble
```

```{r}
# Standalone barplot-only figure (all three fractions side by side)
final_enrichment_figure <- (p_enrich_cellular | p_enrich_soluble | p_enrich_insoluble) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "GO:BP and KEGG enrichment by cluster, ranked by temporal change magnitude",
    subtitle = "Top 10 terms per cluster within each fraction; GO:BP terms rrvgo-reduced; mean |log2 range D14\u2192M18| of intersecting genes (FDR < 0.05)",
    tag_levels = "A"
  ) &
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 15, colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5, colour = "black"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey30")
  )

final_enrichment_figure

ggsave("anova_enrichment_barplots_only.pdf", final_enrichment_figure,
       width = 24, height = 13, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("anova_enrichment_barplots_only.png", final_enrichment_figure,
       width = 24, height = 13, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

## 4. Combined figure (trajectories + enrichment stacked, per fraction)

```{r}
col_cellular  <- p_traj_cellular  / p_enrich_cellular  + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2.2))
col_soluble   <- p_traj_soluble   / p_enrich_soluble   + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2.2))
col_insoluble <- p_traj_insoluble / p_enrich_insoluble + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2.2))
```


combined figure

```{r}
final_combined_figure <- (col_cellular | col_soluble | col_insoluble) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Temporal protein trajectories and cluster-level GO:BP/KEGG enrichment",
    subtitle = "Each fraction clustered independently (silhouette-selected k); GO:BP terms rrvgo-reduced; enrichment ranked by mean temporal change of intersecting genes (FDR < 0.05)",
    tag_levels = "A"
  ) &
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 15, colour = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(family = "Liberation Serif", face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5, colour = "black"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(family = "Carlito", face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey30")
  )

final_combined_figure

ggsave("trajectories_and_enrichment_combined_reduced.pdf", final_combined_figure,
       width = 32, height = 16, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("trajectories_and_enrichment_combined_reduced.png", final_combined_figure,
       width = 32, height = 16, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```
